comments: Approved. The drafted contract is tightly bounded to a design-only outcome: generating tasks/v0.7-m4-environment-adaptation.md that defines environment discovery via fingerprints, capability mapping, drift analysis, and non-intrusive adapter adaptation rules. This matches the recon packet's analytical scope, which explicitly limits M4 to the environment fingerprint model, capability registry, compatibility/drift analysis method, and adapter abstraction rules. The scope language is also appropriately specific about identifying tools through passive fingerprints such as process.env.OPENCODE_SESSION and modeling capabilities such as native MCP support, while keeping the work at the design-document level rather than runtime implementation. The out_of_scope section is correctly restrictive and aligns with both the recon packet and broader V0.7 milestone materials: it forbids actual discovery or adapter implementation in MCP Server/CLI code, forbids rewriting existing adapter templates, forbids locking the protocol to a single toolchain, and forbids workflow state semantics modifications. Allowed files, required outputs, and stop conditions further reinforce that this phase must stop before crossing into implementation, template rewrites, or workflow-state mutation. I found no contract language that authorizes actual adapter code, single-toolchain commitment, or workflow state changes, so the contract is consistent with the intended M4 boundary.
